description: Rectifier diode — blocks reverse current, passes forward current above ~0.7V (silicon) or ~0.3V (Schottky)

absolute_max.forward_current: depends on part (common: 1A–3A for general purpose)

pins.name: A
pins.description: Anode — current enters here in forward bias
pins.requirements: Connect to more positive potential for forward bias operation

pins.name: K
pins.description: Cathode — marked with a band on the body. Current exits here in forward bias.
pins.requirements: Connect to more negative potential for forward bias. The cathode band MUST face the correct direction or the diode blocks instead of passes.

application_circuits.name: Reverse polarity protection
application_circuits.description: Series diode between supply and circuit. Blocks reverse polarity. Forward voltage drop reduces supply slightly.

application_circuits.name: Flyback diode
application_circuits.description: Place across inductive load (motor, relay coil) with cathode toward positive rail to clamp inductive kickback.

common_mistakes: Installing backwards — cathode (band) must face the correct direction
Silicon forward voltage ~0.7V causes voltage drop in power supplies — use Schottky (0.3V) if drop matters
Exceeding peak reverse voltage causes avalanche breakdown and permanent damage
Flyback diodes must be placed physically close to the inductive load
notes: Common parts: 1N4001-1N4007 (50V-1000V, 1A silicon), 1N5819 (40V, 1A Schottky), 1N5822 (40V, 3A Schottky). Schottky diodes have lower forward voltage and faster switching but lower reverse voltage rating.
